behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names the module path, class, methods (send/asend), attributes (_thread_lock, _async_lock), and types (_RequestFrame, _ResponseFrame, VariableResult) with construction forms used in tests.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes the bug (crossed responses) and required behavior but doesn't state the specific fix (moving the return inside the lock block).

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests verify concurrent behavior via socket I/O and response correlation, not specific code structure; alternative fixes (e.g., per-request response queues) would also pass.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests instantiate CommsDecoder, call send() across threads, use real socketpair and msgspec encoding to verify behavior.

test_not_tautological.reason: Concurrent tests assert per-thread key/value correspondence; a stub returning None would fail `assert results[i] is not None` and key/value checks.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Thread safety for send (and implicitly asend via attribute check), ruff checks, and repo test passing are all covered.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped into TestConcurrentSendBehavior, TestCommsDecoderBasic, TestRepoTests classes with docstrings.

structured_data_schema.reason: No structured data output required beyond API shapes documented in instruction.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction identifies the class file but does not point at the specific buggy line.
